channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: The 'vibe coding spectrum' approach to AI-assisted software development
item.description: Different code deserves different levels of oversight, so calibrate your approach to ‘vibe coding’ accordingly.

item.title: The Scripts on Your Checkout Page Are Now a PCI DSS Problem
item.description: An independent PCI assessor tested Reflectiz against the new PCI DSS rules. Here is the verdict: See the full QSA assessment here → When a customer types their card number into your checkout, their browser is running far more than your code. Analytics tags, a tag manager, a support widget, a...

item.title: Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description: Splunk patched an OS command injection in AI Toolkit, while Atlassian fixed dozens of flaws in third-party dependencies. The post Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications
item.description: The Android malware allows its operators to take control of infected devices and harvest sensitive information. The post Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE
item.description: Insufficient validation of user input allows an attacker to gain access to the underlying OS and elevate their privileges to root. The post Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Geoengineering still faces major practical challenges
item.description: Solar geoengineering is often portrayed as a sort of emergency brake. Something along the lines of Pull in case of climate emergency to scatter light-reflecting particles to bounce sunlight out of the atmosphere and cool the planet. But it might be less like a simple brake and more like a...

item.title: SSO login errors for some ChatGPT Enterprise workspaces
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Login (Degraded performance)

item.title: F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities
item.description: Critical flaws in NGINX could allow remote, unauthenticated attackers to cause a restart and potentially execute arbitrary code. The post F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The search for dark matter has been blown wide open
item.description: Underneath an Apennine massif, below the Jinping Mountains of Sichuan, and at the bottom of a South Dakota mine, there is a cosmic hunt afoot. Isolated deep beneath these rocky shields, massive detectors filled with liquid xenon aim to make the first direct detections of dark matter, the...

item.title: SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal
item.description: Israel-based Entro specializes in non-human identity and credential security solutions, and it will enable SailPoint to enhance its products. The post SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Service disruption on Claude services
item.description: Jun 18, 08:19 UTC Resolved - The issue affecting Claude services has been resolved. Impact occurred from 06:55 to 07:40 UTC. Jun 18, 07:41 UTC Identified - We have identified the cause of the issue affecting Claude.ai and are working on a fix. We will provide an update as soon as possible.

item.title: Loop Engineering: The New Division of Labor in the Agent Era
item.description: Loop Engineering emerges as the successor to Prompt Engineering, shifting developers from manually instructing AI agents to designing self-sustaining automated systems.

item.title: AI Winds Reach the Panel Industry: TCL Technology Due for a Revaluation
item.description: TCL Technology stands to benefit from surging AI demand for glass substrates in semiconductor packaging, as display panel expertise finds new applications in the AI chip supply chain.

item.title: From Perfect Gaokao Math Score to AI-Assisted College Applications: How Far Have China's LLMs Come?
item.description: Tencent Yuanbao achieves a perfect 150/150 on the 2026 Gaokao math exam and demonstrates AI agent capabilities in college application guidance, showcasing Chinese LLM advancement.

item.title: HDC 2026: HarmonyOS 7 Developer Beta Launches with 66 Million Terminal Ecosystem
item.description: Huawei's HDC 2026 unveils HarmonyOS 7 Developer Beta, deeply integrating AI at the OS level with 66 million consumer devices and a rapidly growing developer ecosystem.

item.title: Andrew Ng: AI Hype Aside, Future Companies Will Be 10-Person Teams Rebuilding Data Architecture with Agents
item.description: Andrew Ng discusses at LangChain Interrupt how 10-person engineering teams with AI agents will drive enterprise transformation, with data architecture as the critical foundation.

item.title: [AINews] Midjourney Medical: scan your organs like you step on a scale
item.description: The only bootstrapped frontier lab announces its second product and second

item.title: Quantifying Consistency in LLM Logical Reasoning via Structural Uncertainty
item.description: arXiv:2606.17312v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models can arrive at the same answer through reasoning paths that are unstable, contradictory, or difficult to rank consistently -- a failure mode especially prevalent in multi-step deductive reasoning. Existing methods assess...

item.title: MemTrace: Probing What Final Accuracy Misses in Long-Term Memory
item.description: arXiv:2606.17328v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM agents increasingly maintain long-term memory of user facts across sessions. Yet such memory is usually evaluated by aggregating accuracy over question rows or episodes. Because this approach scores question rows independently, even when several...

item.title: SpeechDx: A Multi-Task Benchmark for Clinical Speech AI
item.description: arXiv:2606.17339v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speech offers a uniquely informative window into health by simultaneously engaging neurological, motor, respiratory, and vocal systems. Current clinical speech AI methods have largely progressed through isolated condition-specific studies, making...

item.title: Distributed General-Purpose Agent Networks: Architecture, Key Mechanisms, and Prototypes
item.description: arXiv:2606.17368v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models have accelerated the transition from passive conversational assistants to autonomous agents that can understand goals, plan actions, invoke tools, and execute multi-step tasks. Yet the capability of a single agent remains...

item.title: Treatment Response Optimized Clinical Decision Support AI System via Digital Twin Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2606.17405v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Clinical decision support AI systems (CDSASs) must adapt to evolving patient conditions in real-time while adhering to strict safety constraints. We present an online adaptive framework that integrates Treatment Effect (TE) estimation to quantify...

item.title: Gaussian Mixture Attention: Linear-Time Sequence Mixing via Probabilistic Latent Routing
item.description: arXiv:2606.18283v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The dense token-to-token interaction pattern of standard dot-product attention remains a central bottleneck in scaling Transformer architectures to long contexts. We introduce \textbf{Gaussian Mixture Attention (GMA)}, a probabilistic attention-style...

item.title: Breaking the Solver Bottleneck: Training Task Generators at the Learnable Frontier
item.description: arXiv:2606.18284v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The limiting resource for training agents via reinforcement learning (RL) is increasingly frontier task supply: valid, solvable tasks just difficult enough to train the current model. As reasoning and agentic models improve, fixed task distributions...

item.title: CODEBLOCK: Learning to Supervise Code at the Right Granularity
item.description: arXiv:2606.18286v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Supervised fine-tuning of code LLMs typically applies uniform cross-entropy loss to all response tokens, implicitly assuming that every token provides equally useful learning signal. Recent token-level selection methods challenge this assumption in...

item.title: Artemis: Anatomy-Resolved inTervention for Eliminating Multimodal NeuroImage confounderS
item.description: arXiv:2606.18287v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal neuroimaging, integrating functional connectivity from fMRI and structural connectivity from DTI, enables non-invasive analysis of brain networks using graph neural networks. However, demographic factors such as age and sex systematically...

item.title: A Link between Shock-wave Theory and Symmetry-reduced Stochastic Gradient Descent for Artificial Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2606.18303v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We develop a mathematically explicit link between shock-wave theory and the symmetry-quotiented learning dynamics of stochastic gradient descent, drawing on differential geometry, Lie group theory, and fluid mechanics. Specifically, after quotienting...

item.title: Continuous Audio Thinking for Large Audio Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.18273v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large audio language models (LALMs) have shown impressive capabilities on diverse audio understanding tasks, ranging from speech transcription to music analysis. However, because LALMs are typically trained to produce text-aligned responses, their...

item.title: Redact or Keep? A Fully Local AI Cascade for Educational Dialogue De-Identification
item.description: arXiv:2606.18372v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Educational dialogue is a valuable but sensitive resource for research: the same transcripts that capture authentic learning often capture personally identifiable information (PII) entangled with curricular content, where "Riemann" may refer to a real...

item.title: SproutRAG: Attention-Guided Tree Search with Progressive Embeddings for Long-Document RAG
item.description: arXiv:2606.18381v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems must balance retrieval granularity with contextual coherence, a challenge that existing methods address through LLM-guided chunking, single-level context expansion, or hierarchical summarization. These...

item.title: Want Better Synthetic Data? Steer It: Activation Steering for Low-Resource Language Generation
item.description: arXiv:2606.18389v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become an effective tool for synthetic data generation, including for low-resource languages, where generated data can improve downstream task performance. Current best-performing approaches typically rely on few-shot...

item.title: JetFlow: Breaking the Scaling Ceiling of Speculative Decoding with Parallel Tree Drafting
item.description: arXiv:2606.18394v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding (SD) accelerates autoregressive Large Language Models (LLMs) by drafting multiple tokens and verifying them in parallel, but it faces a scaling limitation: increasing the draft budget improves speed only when acceptance remains...

item.title: ChatGPT failing to load or save
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Agent (Operational) Login (Operational) Voice mode (Operational) Compliance API (Operational) Image Generation (Operational) GPTs (Operational) Deep Research (Operational) Connectors/Apps (Operational) ChatGPT Atlas...

item.title: Snippets requests returning Worker threw exception
item.description: Jun 18, 01:52 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 18, 01:44 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 18, 00:46 UTC Identified - Cloudflare is aware of an issue impacting a small subset of customers where Snippets may be returning "Worker...

item.title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
item.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed leveraging paid or promoted posts on legitimate news websites to drum up buzz for their warez, according to new findings from Check Point Research. The threat actor also has at their disposal a dedicated WordPress phishing page that acts as the central hub,...

item.title: Browser Isolation Availability Issues
item.description: Jun 17, 18:12 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 17, 17:08 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating an issue with the Browser Isolation service. Impact is limited to customers accessing websites secured with Cloudflare Turnstile, who may...

item.title: 🔬 The Self-Driving Lab — Joseph Krause, Radical AI
item.description: Radical AI's Joseph Krause on why the moat in materials is the lab, not the model

item.title: Disruption with Copilot next edit suggestions
item.description: Jun 17, 17:57 UTC Investigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: The Chandra-Gaia Catalog of Counterparts: Resolving ambiguous Gaia matches to X-ray sources in the Chandra Source Catalog using Machine Learning
item.description: We present a framework to cross-match sources from the Chandra Source Catalog (CSC v2.1) with optical sources from Gaia Data Release 3. Unlike purely spatial approaches, we use source properties such as magnitudes, colors, and distances to identify true counterparts, detect chance coincidences, and...

item.title: Reference-Driven Multi-Speaker Audio Scene Generation from In-the-Wild Priors
item.description: Existing multi-speaker dialogue systems bind speakers to utterances through structured supervision: per-turn tags, multi-stream transcriptions, or learnable speaker embeddings. These systems operate within speech-only pipelines that produce clean vocal sequences without the ambient texture of real...

item.title: Data Intelligence Agents: Interpreting, Modeling, and Querying Enterprise Data via Autonomous Coding Agents
item.description: Production data integration is bottlenecked by repeated, lossy handoffs between data owners, engineers, and analysts who must collaboratively discover, structure, and query enterprise data. We present Data Intelligence Agents (DIA), a system of three agents (Data Interpreter, Schema Creator, and...

item.title: Errors with conversations on Android and iOS devices
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Conversations (Partial outage)

item.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
item.description: Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet. The vulnerability has now been assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), with the tech giant describing it as a privilege escalation flaw. "Microsoft is...

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jun 17, 16:28 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 17, 16:01 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 17, 15:34 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Junior Hacker Used Tailscale and OpenSSH to Keep Access After His C2 Went Offline
item.description: A French-speaking attacker broke into a small French automotive business, planted a keylogger, and stole banking and email credentials. Ordinary stuff, until one move near the end. Before his command-and-control server went dark, he installed OpenSSH and Tailscale on a victim's machine, building a...

item.title: MolmoMotion: Language-guided 3D motion forecasting
State of the blog, mid-2026
item.description: About 3 years since I started writing weekly.

item.title: Cloudflare Network Interconnect 2.0 BGP Route Propagation Issues
item.description: Jun 17, 12:13 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 17, 11:34 UTC Identified - Cloudflare Network Interconnect customers using may experience issues related to BGP functionality and route propagation. The issue has been identified and a fix is being...

item.title: The Download: a reality check for geoengineering and the science of interoception
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Hacking the atmosphere: geoengineering gets a reality check Solar geoengineering, the controversial idea that we could deliberately intervene in the...

item.title: NCSC CEO: Hostile states linked to three-quarters of cyber attacks affecting UK's critical systems
item.description: Dr Richard Horne highlighted the scale of cyber threats against the UK’s critical infrastructure at RUSI’s Annual Security Lecture.
